notes: Iteration 4 verifies 3 improvements: (1) detect-observability.sh now detects project-specific telemetry dirs, (2) richer templates (import-direction lint, dep-graph, CI pipeline), (3) ASSESS-ONLY fast path. All evals target Open-ClaudeCode (same as iteration 3).

evals.prompt: Assess the scaffolding state of /Users/josh_folder/Open-ClaudeCode and tell me what's missing for autonomous agent operation

analyst_observations: PASS RATE: with_skill improved from 93.3% to 100% — the detects-existing-telemetry fix worked. The key failure from iter3 (eval-1 with_skill missed telemetry) is now fixed.
SPEED: with_skill is now 22% faster than baseline on average (360s vs 465s), up from near-parity in iter3 (0.7%). The ASSESS-ONLY mode and improved probes reduced overhead significantly.
EVAL-2 ASSESS-ONLY: The with_skill assess-only run completed in 199s (vs 252s in iter3 without assess-only mode). That's a 21% speedup from skipping the GENERATE/RE-ASSESS loop.
EVAL-3 OBSERVABILITY: with_skill is 35% faster than baseline (340s vs 525s). The skill produces focused shell scripts; the baseline produces richer TypeScript source files. Both pass all assertions.
NEW TEMPLATES: All 3 new templates (lint-import-direction.sh, analyze-deps.sh, ci-pipeline.yml) were generated and used in eval-1 with_skill. The baseline also independently created similar scripts.

TELEMETRY DETECTION: The improved detect-observability.sh correctly found src/utils/telemetry/ (9 files) and src/services/analytics/ (9 files). This boosted O1-O4 scores to Level 3 in all with_skill runs.
BASELINE IMPROVEMENT: without_skill pass rate also improved (80% → 93.3%), likely due to natural variance. The skill's advantage is now in speed (+22%) and consistency (100% vs 93.3%).

REMAINING GAP: The without_skill baseline consistently produces more creative/project-specific output (e.g., PII detection, telemetry pipeline monitoring). The skill's template-based approach is more consistent but less creative.
